definition.text: A small fastener on clothing used to

example.text: The shirt was unwearable because three
s were missing from the front

words.tips: Common clothing complaint. Always check buttons before purchasing—loose buttons indicate poor quality stitching.

definition.text: An expression of dissatisfaction about a product, service, or
problem that needs to be resolved

example.text: The store manager listened carefully to my
about the broken zipper and offered an immediate replacement

words.tips: Effective complaints state the problem clearly, provide evidence (receipts), and specify desired resolution (refund/repair).

definition.text: A person who purchases goods or services for
rather than for business resale; an

example.text: , she always reads product reviews before making expensive purchases

words.tips: Consumer rights include safety, information, choice, and being heard. Know your rights before filing complaints.

definition.text: A shallow depression or hollow caused by impact on a hard surface like metal, without

example.text: The delivery truck left a deep
in my car door when the driver wasn't paying attention

words.tips: Common car/appliance complaint. Small dents may be repairable with suction tools; larger ones require professional bodywork.

definition.text: The handle used to open or close a door; often round and designed to be

example.text: came off completely in my hand, trapping me inside until maintenance arrived

words.tips: Loose doorknobs are common in older buildings. Usually fixed by tightening the setscrew on the neck of the knob.

definition.text: The machine that provides power to move a vehicle or operate machinery by converting fuel or electricity into

example.text: began making a loud knocking noise after driving through deep floodwater

words.tips: Major repair expense. Always get written estimates from multiple mechanics before authorizing engine work.

definition.text: Material made by weaving, knitting, or felting fibers together to create

example.text: The expensive jacket was ruined when the cheap
faded unevenly after just one wash

words.tips: Check fabric content labels before buying. Natural fibers (cotton, wool) breathe better but may shrink; synthetics (polyester) resist wrinkles but trap heat.

definition.text: A valve controlling the release of water from a pipe; the fixture on sinks, bathtubs, or showers used to
turn water flow on and off

example.text: has been dripping nonstop for three days, wasting water and driving me crazy

words.tips: American English: faucet. British English: tap. Most dripping faucets are fixed by replacing a worn rubber washer inside the handle.

definition.text: An opening or gap in a surface where material is

example.text: in my new jeans after sitting on a rusty nail at the park

words.tips: Different from 'tear' (ripped edges still attached) or 'stain' (discoloration without missing material). Small holes in clothing can often be invisibly mended.

definition.text: Buildings or structures where people live; residential

example.text: with basic amenities, though maintenance requests are often ignored

words.tips: In complaints context: 'housing' often refers to rental properties. Tenants have rights regarding habitability (working plumbing, heat, safe structure).

definition.text: Materials used to wrap, contain, or protect a product during

example.text: The fragile vase arrived shattered because the inadequate
provided no cushioning against impacts during shipping

words.tips: Excessive packaging creates waste; insufficient packaging causes damage. Take photos of damaged items inside original packaging before filing claims.

definition.text: A long, hollow cylinder used to transport liquids or gases; in buildings, pipes carry
waste through walls and under floors

example.text: burst in the basement during the cold snap, flooding the entire lower level

words.tips: Common housing complaint in winter. Prevent frozen pipes by letting faucets drip slightly during extreme cold and insulating exposed pipes.

definition.text: The return of money paid for a product or service that was

example.text: After the defective headphones stopped working after two days, the store issued a full

words.tips: Know return policies before purchasing. Keep receipts. Some stores offer store credit instead of cash refunds—ask for your preference.

definition.text: To fix something that is broken or damaged so it functions or looks

example.text: the cracked phone screen tomorrow morning for a reasonable fee

words.tips: Get written estimates before authorizing repairs. Ask if parts are new or refurbished. Some repairs cost more than replacement—do the math first.

definition.text: A physical store or location where goods are sold directly to
rather than to other businesses; a

example.text: The defective blender had to be returned to the original
where it was purchased, not to any store in the chain

words.tips: Different from 'online retailer' (website-only sales). Some manufacturers require returns at original purchase location for warranty claims.

definition.text: The display surface of electronic devices like phones, tablets, or TVs where images and text are
; also a mesh barrier that keeps

example.text: cracked when I dropped it on the tile floor, making half the display unreadable

words.tips: Most common electronics complaint. Screen protectors are cheap insurance. Check if device has 'cracked screen' coverage under warranty (usually excluded).

definition.text: A wave or pulse that carries information wirelessly; the strength of connection for phones, internet, or TV that allows

example.text: My phone shows zero bars of
in my apartment, making it impossible to make calls without going to the balcony

words.tips: Weak signals cause dropped calls and slow internet. Solutions: Wi-Fi calling, signal boosters, or contacting provider about coverage gaps in your area.

definition.text: The part of a garment that covers the arm, extending from the shoulder to the

example.text: was torn at the seam after catching on a door handle

words.tips: Common wear point on clothing. Reinforced seams at sleeve caps indicate better quality construction. Torn sleeves are usually repairable by a tailor.

definition.text: A discolored mark left on fabric, carpet, or surfaces by spilled liquids, dirt, or other substances that

example.text: on the white tablecloth that wouldn't come out even after professional cleaning

words.tips: Act quickly on stains—blot (don't rub!) with cold water. Different stains need different treatments: grease (dish soap), ink (alcohol), wine (salt then vinegar).

definition.text: A skilled worker trained to repair, maintain, or operate specialized equipment; a
in a technical field like electronics, appliances, or

example.text: diagnosed the refrigerator problem in five minutes and fixed it with a $15 part

words.tips: Always verify technician credentials and get written estimates. Reputable technicians explain the problem and repair options before starting work.

definition.text: A written guarantee from a manufacturer promising to repair or replace a product if it fails due to defects within a specified

example.text: The laptop stopped working after ten months, but thankfully it was still under
so the company replaced it free of charge

words.tips: Register products online to activate warranties. Keep receipts and warranty cards. 'Full warranty' covers everything; 'limited warranty' has exclusions (like accidental damage).

definition.text: A single sheet of glass set in a window frame; the transparent surface that allows light in while keeping weather

example.text: A baseball crashed through the living room
during the neighborhood kids' game, leaving a perfect circle of broken glass

words.tips: Different from 'window' (the entire structure including frame). Single-pane windows are less energy efficient than double-pane. Most home insurance covers accidental window breakage.

definition.text: Damaged so that it no longer works or is in

example.text: The delivery person left my new lamp on the porch and the glass shade arrived completely

words.tips: Most serious condition descriptor. Different from 'cracked' (still holds together) or 'damaged' (partial impairment).

definition.text: Having thin lines where the surface has

example.text: I dropped my phone in the parking lot and now the screen is badly

words.tips: Often repairable if caught early. Different from 'broken'—cracked items may still function but are weakened and unsightly.

definition.text: Harmed or impaired so that quality, appearance, or function is

example.text: The sofa arrived with the armrest badly
— deep scratches and torn fabric from rough handling during shipping

words.tips: Broad term covering various levels of harm. More specific terms (cracked, torn, scratched) often better for precise complaints.

definition.text: Having completely lost electrical power or charge; no longer able to
function due to lack of energy

example.text: after just six months—no amount of charging would bring them back to life

words.tips: Primarily used for batteries/devices. Not the same as 'broken' (mechanical failure) vs. 'dead' (power failure). Often indicates end of product lifespan.

definition.text: Allowing liquid to fall in drops continuously; leaking water slowly but

example.text: all night long—a steady plink-plink-plink that kept me awake

words.tips: Wastes significant water over time (a dripping faucet can waste hundreds of gallons monthly). Usually fixed by replacing a worn rubber washer.

definition.text: Having lost brightness or color intensity through washing, sun exposure, or wear; appearing

example.text: after one summer of direct sunlight, turning from rich blue to a dull grayish tone

words.tips: Can be intentional (distressed fashion) or unintentional damage. Check care labels—some fabrics require UV protection or gentle washing to prevent fading.

definition.text: Extremely dirty or unclean; covered in grime, dust, stains, or

example.text: The vacation rental looked clean in photos, but the bathroom tiles were absolutely
with hair clogged in the drain and mold growing in the corners

words.tips: Strong complaint word implying unacceptable conditions. More intense than 'dirty'—suggests health/sanitation concerns. Document with photos before reporting.

definition.text: Having completely lost air pressure; deflated and unable to support weight or

example.text: tire on the highway during rush hour and had to wait an hour for roadside assistance

words.tips: Caused by punctures, valve leaks, or bead separation. Always carry a spare tire and know how to change it. 'Flat' here is adjective (not noun—'a flat' is British slang for apartment).

definition.text: Remaining whole and undamaged; not broken, spoiled, or

example.text: Miraculously, the fragile vase arrived completely
despite the shipping box being crushed on one side

words.tips: Useful contrast word in complaints: 'The packaging was damaged, but the product arrived intact.' Opposite of broken/damaged/cracked.

definition.text: Allowing liquid or gas to escape through small holes, cracks, or faulty seals; not

example.text: pipe that's causing water stains to spread across our living room ceiling

words.tips: Common housing complaint. 'Leaky' describes the faulty condition; 'leaking' describes the active process. Address immediately to prevent mold and structural damage.

definition.text: Not firmly fixed in place; able to move, wobble, or come undone because it is

example.text: The kitchen cabinet door became dangerously
after months of use and now swings open unexpectedly when we open adjacent drawers

words.tips: Usually fixed by tightening screws or replacing worn parts. Safety hazard when affecting stairs, railings, or heavy furniture.

definition.text: Cut or designed to hang away from the body without hugging curves; having
through hips, thighs, or torso—may be intentional style or unintended

example.text: I ordered a medium sweater but received an extremely
one that looks like it would fit someone two sizes larger

words.tips: Distinguish between intentional style ('loose-fitting fashion') vs. defect ('wrong size received'). Check product descriptions before complaining—some items are designed to be oversized.

definition.text: Not present or accounted for; absent when it should be included or attached; having

example.text: The furniture assembly kit was unusable because three essential screws were

words.tips: Common complaint for products with multiple parts. Always inventory contents immediately upon opening. Take photos of missing items next to packaging/label for evidence.

definition.text: Marked with thin, shallow cuts or grooves on the surface; damaged by something sharp dragging across it without

example.text: after I accidentally dropped them lens-down on the pavement

words.tips: Different from 'cracked' (split through material) or 'dented' (indented surface). Light scratches on glass may be polished out; deep ones usually require replacement.

definition.text: Joined or repaired by stitching with needle and thread; having been

example.text: The tailor returned my jacket with the torn seam perfectly
using thread that matched the original color

words.tips: Past participle of 'sew' (irregular verb: sew → sewed/sewn). 'Sewn' emphasizes the completed state of being stitched. Not the same as 'sewed' (simple past tense action).

definition.text: Marked with a discolored spot where liquid, dirt, or other substances have penetrated the surface, changing its

example.text: after red wine spilled and wasn't treated immediately

words.tips: Act quickly on fresh stains—blot (don't rub!) with cold water. Set-in stains may require professional cleaning. Some fabrics (silk, wool) need special stain treatments.

definition.text: Ripped apart with visible separation where edges have been pulled away from each other; fabric or material that is

example.text: after catching on a sharp fence post during my morning run

words.tips: Different from 'hole' (missing material without torn edges) or 'worn' (thinned by use). Most torn clothing can be repaired by sewing unless severely shredded.

definition.text: A reassuring expression used to calm someone who is anxious, upset, or concerned; intended to
and convey that a situation is

example.text: "I'm so sorry I broke your favorite mug!" "
I've been meaning to replace it anyway—it had a hairline crack already."

words.tips: Very common in customer service to de-escalate complaints. Tone matters—said sincerely it comforts; said dismissively it can anger customers further.

definition.text: Used to express an assumption or conclusion based on what someone has described; showing you're

example.text: "The engine makes a grinding noise when I turn left, and smoke comes from under the hood." "
you might have a serious transmission problem—better get it checked today."

words.tips: Shows empathy and active listening. Leaves room for correction ('It sounds like X, but maybe I'm misunderstanding'). Very useful in complaint resolution.

definition.text: An extremely polite way to accept an offer of help or service; softens the request by making it

synonyms: Yes, thank you, if it's not too much trouble

example.text: "Would you like me to carry those heavy boxes upstairs for you?" "
That would be a huge help—my back has been bothering me all day."

words.tips: More polite than a simple 'yes'—ideal for customer service interactions and formal situations. The 'if you could' makes it optional rather than demanding.

definition.text: To examine something carefully to discover its

example.text: Before buying a used car, always
the engine, tires, and interior for hidden damage

words.tips: Essential first step in any complaint process. Document problems with photos before reporting issues to sellers or landlords.

definition.text: To stop working suddenly and completely, especially for computers or electronic devices; to

example.text: in the middle of saving my important report, and I lost three hours of work

words.tips: Common electronics complaint. Always save work frequently (Ctrl+S) and enable auto-save features to minimize data loss during crashes.

definition.text: that would shrink or lose shape in a washing machine

example.text: only'—never put it in your washing machine or it will shrink permanently

words.tips: Always check clothing labels before washing. 'Dry-clean only' means water will damage the fabric. Many 'dry-clean' items can actually be hand-washed gently—but test first.

definition.text: To buy something; the act of acquiring goods or services by

example.text: these headphones online last week, but they arrived with a cracked case

words.tips: Formal term often used in receipts, warranties, and legal contexts. Keep purchase receipts—they're essential proof when filing complaints or warranty claims.

definition.text: To deliberately avoid doing something; to
from an action, especially one that might be

example.text: raising your voice—even if you're frustrated—to get better results

words.tips: Always followed by '-ing' verb: 'refrain from shouting', NOT 'refrain to shout'. Formal expression often seen in warnings: 'Please refrain from smoking'.

definition.text: To apply a fresh coat of paint over an existing surface to

example.text: the apartment walls after the previous tenant left large scuff marks near the doors

words.tips: Common housing complaint resolution. Proper repainting requires surface preparation (cleaning, sanding, priming) for long-lasting results—not just slapping on new paint.

definition.text: To fix something that is broken or damaged so it works or looks

example.text: the broken zipper on my jacket under warranty rather than giving me a refund

words.tips: Get written estimates before authorizing repairs. Ask if parts are new or refurbished. Sometimes repair costs exceed replacement value—always compare options.

definition.text: To join pieces of fabric together using

example.text: My grandmother taught me how to
a button back onto a shirt in under a minute using a simple cross-stitch pattern

words.tips: Basic life skill that saves money on clothing repairs. Keep a small sewing kit with needles, thread, and spare buttons. Past tense: sewed or sewn (sewn is more common as past participle: 'a sewn patch').

definition.text: To make a blade or point
the edge against an abrasive surface

example.text: your kitchen knives monthly—they're actually more dangerous when dull because they require more force to cut

words.tips: Applies to knives, pencils, scissors, and even skills ('sharpen your math skills'). Use a honing steel regularly to maintain edge alignment; use a whetstone for actual sharpening when blades become dull.
